# How decisive were these results, combined?

Seven animal kingdom grudge matches this week.


::: {.cell}

:::


**TL;DR:** Hedgehogs steamrolled Porcupines (45–18, 71%), the most one-sided result of the week. Crocodylia was the nail-biter: Alligator over Crocodile by just 5 votes (p = 0.61) — essentially a coin flip between the two reptiles.


::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}


|question      |  N|Leader (votes) |Trailer (votes) |Leader share | Margin (votes)|Margin (points) | p vs 50/50|Leader 95% CI | Votes to flip|
|:-------------|--:|:--------------|:---------------|:------------|--------------:|:---------------|----------:|:-------------|-------------:|
|Anura         | 63|Frog (42)      |Toad (21)       |66.7%        |             21|33.3%           |   0.011100|[0.54, 0.78]  |            11|
|Corvidae      | 62|Raven (41)     |Crow (21)       |66.1%        |             20|32.3%           |   0.015100|[0.53, 0.78]  |            11|
|Crocodylia    | 61|Alligator (33) |Crocodile (28)  |54.1%        |              5|8.2%            |   0.609000|[0.41, 0.67]  |             3|
|Insect-Eaters | 61|Armadillo (35) |Pangolin (26)   |57.4%        |              9|14.8%           |   0.306000|[0.44, 0.70]  |             5|
|Lepidoptera   | 64|Butterfly (44) |Moth (20)       |68.8%        |             24|37.5%           |   0.003690|[0.56, 0.80]  |            13|
|Pinnipeds     | 61|Seal (34)      |Sea Lion (27)   |55.7%        |              7|11.5%           |   0.443000|[0.42, 0.68]  |             4|
|Spiky         | 63|Hedgehog (45)  |Porcupine (18)  |71.4%        |             27|42.9%           |   0.000898|[0.59, 0.82]  |            14|


:::
:::



::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![Leader shares with 95% confidence intervals, ranked from most lopsided to closest.](index_files/figure-html/fig-leader-ci-1.png){#fig-leader-ci width=768}
:::
:::


# Does HQ agree with RTP?


::: {.cell}

:::



::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}


|question      |Leader (overall) |HQ share    |RTP share   |Gap (pts) | p-value|
|:-------------|:----------------|:-----------|:-----------|:---------|-------:|
|Anura         |Frog             |71% (24/34) |62% (18/29) |9%        |   0.655|
|Corvidae      |Raven            |65% (22/34) |68% (19/28) |3%        |   1.000|
|Crocodylia    |Alligator        |52% (17/33) |57% (16/28) |6%        |   0.856|
|Insect-Eaters |Armadillo        |64% (21/33) |50% (14/28) |14%       |   0.416|
|Lepidoptera   |Butterfly        |74% (25/34) |63% (19/30) |10%       |   0.543|
|Pinnipeds     |Seal             |53% (18/34) |59% (16/27) |6%        |   0.815|
|Spiky         |Hedgehog         |71% (24/34) |72% (21/29) |2%        |   1.000|


:::
:::



::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![HQ vs RTP support for each question's overall winner.](index_files/figure-html/fig-hq-rtp-1.png){#fig-hq-rtp width=672}
:::
:::



::: {.cell}

:::


None of the individual gaps clear statistical significance this week (all p > 0.28) — but the week-over-week Alignment Index takes its biggest tumble yet, down to **0.47** (cumulative, smoothed over both RTP-era polls so far: **0.89**). No single matchup is a smoking gun; it looks more like everything drifted slightly in the same direction at once.

The widest gap belongs to Insect-Eaters, where HQ leaned toward Armadillo (64%) while RTP was almost perfectly torn — RTP's raw vote on that question split exactly down the middle. It's a good reminder that "the office average" can paper over a genuine local difference of opinion, even when it's not big enough to call statistically significant yet.
